node --import tsx/esm -e "import React, { useRef } from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useClipDrag } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.Element = dom.window.Element; globalThis.Node = dom.window.Node; globalThis.PointerEvent = dom.window.MouseEvent; const selectCalls = []; const coordinator = { update: () => ({ time: 0, rowIndex: 0, trackId: 'V1', trackKind: 'visual', trackName: 'V1', isNewTrack: false, isReject: false, newTrackKind: null, screenCoords: { rowTop: 0, rowLeft: 0, rowWidth: 0, rowHeight: 48, clipLeft: 0, clipWidth: 0, ghostCenter: 0 } }), end: () => undefined, get lastPosition(){ return null; } }; function App(){ const timelineWrapperRef = useRef(null); const dataRef = useRef({ rows: [{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 0, end: 1, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }] }], tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }] }); useClipDrag({ timelineWrapperRef, dataRef, moveClipToRow: () => undefined, createTrackAndMoveClip: () => undefined, selectClip: (clipId, opts) => selectCalls.push({ clipId, toggle: Boolean(opts?.toggle) }), selectedClipIdsRef: { current: new Set(['clip-1']) }, coordinator, rowHeight: 48, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, startLeft: 0 }); return React.createElement('div', { ref: timelineWrapperRef }, React.createElement('button', { className: 'clip-action', 'data-clip-id': 'clip-1', 'data-row-id': 'V1' }, 'clip')); } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); const clip = document.querySelector('.clip-action'); await act(async () => { clip.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerdown', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0 })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerup', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0 })); clip.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerdown', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 2, button: 0, ctrlKey: true })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerup', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 2, button: 0, ctrlKey: true })); }); console.log(JSON.stringify(selectCalls)); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

node --import tsx/esm -e "import React, { useRef } from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useMarqueeSelect } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useMarqueeSelect.ts'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.Element = dom.window.Element; globalThis.Node = dom.window.Node; globalThis.PointerEvent = dom.window.MouseEvent; let latestRect = null; const calls = []; function App(){ const editAreaRef = useRef(null); const dragSessionRef = useRef({ active: true }); const marquee = useMarqueeSelect({ editAreaRef, dragSessionRef, selectClips: (ids) => calls.push({ kind: 'select', ids: [...ids] }), addToSelection: (ids) => calls.push({ kind: 'add', ids: [...ids] }) }); latestRect = marquee.marqueeRect; return React.createElement('div', { ref: editAreaRef, onPointerDown: marquee.onPointerDown }, React.createElement('div', { className: 'clip-action', 'data-clip-id': 'clip-1' }, 'clip')); } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); const area = document.querySelector('div'); await act(async () => { area.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerdown', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 10, clientY: 10 })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointermove', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 40, clientY: 40 })); window.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerup', { bubbles: true, pointerId: 1, button: 0, clientX: 40, clientY: 40 })); }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ rect: latestRect, calls })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

node --import tsx/esm -e "import React from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useClipEditing } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; const calls = []; let api; const dataRef = { current: { rows: [{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-a', start: 0, end: 1, effectId: 'a' }, { id: 'clip-b', start: 1, end: 2, effectId: 'b' }] }], meta: { 'clip-a': { track: 'V1', volume: 1 }, 'clip-b': { track: 'V1', volume: 0 } }, resolvedConfig: { output: { resolution: '1280x720', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], clips: [], registry: {} }, clipOrder: {}, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }] } }; function App(){ api = useClipEditing({ dataRef, resolvedConfig: null, selectedClipId: 'clip-a', selectedTrack: null, currentTime: 0, setSelectedClipId: () => undefined, setSelectedTrackId: () => undefined, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => calls.push(args), applyResolvedConfigEdit: () => undefined }); return null; } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); await act(async () => { api.handleDeleteClips(['clip-a']); api.handleToggleMuteClips(['clip-a', 'clip-b']); }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ deleteCall: { rows: calls[0][0], metaDeletes: calls[0][2] }, muteCall: { metaUpdates: calls[1][1] } })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

node --import tsx/esm -e "import React, { useLayoutEffect, useRef } from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useMultiSelect } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; let api; function App(){ const multi = useMultiSelect(); const pruneSelectionRef = useRef(null); useLayoutEffect(() => { pruneSelectionRef.current = multi.pruneSelection; }, [multi.pruneSelection]); api = { multi, pruneSelectionRef }; return null; } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); await act(async () => { api.multi.selectClips(['clip-a', 'clip-b']); api.pruneSelectionRef.current(new Set(['clip-b'])); }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ selected: [...api.multi.selectedClipIds], primary: api.multi.primaryClipId })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

task_updates.executor_notes: Added a bulk settings panel path in PropertiesPanel for multi-selection, showing a clip-count summary plus shared opacity, speed, and mute controls. Mixed values render as placeholders, numeric blur and Enter commits ignore blank drafts, and actual updates flow through the new batch clip meta handler.

Verified the realtime and reload pruning contract without additional code changes by exercising the same pruneSelectionRef bridge shape used in useTimelineState. After selecting multiple clips, pruning against a reloaded valid-id set removed stale clip ids and promoted the surviving clip to primary, leaving no orphaned selection state.
commands_run:
node --import tsx/esm -e "import React, { useLayoutEffect, useRef } from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useMultiSelect } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts'; globalThis.IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; let api; function App(){ const multi = useMultiSelect(); const pruneSelectionRef = useRef(null); useLayoutEffect(() => { pruneSelectionRef.current = multi.pruneSelection; }, [multi.pruneSelection]); api = { multi, pruneSelectionRef }; return null; } const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('app')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(App)); }); await act(async () => { api.multi.selectClips(['clip-a', 'clip-b']); api.pruneSelectionRef.current(new Set(['clip-b'])); }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ selected: [...api.multi.selectedClipIds], primary: api.multi.primaryClipId })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified with a jsdom useClipDrag probe that a plain click emitted selectClip('clip-1') and a ctrl-click emitted selectClip('clip-1', { toggle: true }) from pointer-up, confirming click-selection no longer depends on ClipAction.onClick.

Verified with a jsdom useMarqueeSelect probe that seeding dragSessionRef.current blocks marquee activation entirely; the result stayed { rect: null, calls: [] } even after pointer-down, move, and up on the edit area.

Verified both sides of the delete path: handleDeleteClips filtered the selected clip out of the row set in one applyTimelineEdit call, and a pruneSelectionRef bridge probe left selectedClipIds=['clip-b'] with primary='clip-b', so the UI state does not keep deleted ids.

Verified intentionally that batch mute toggles each clip individually. The probe returned meta updates { clip-a: { volume: 0 }, clip-b: { volume: 1 } } for a mixed selection, rather than forcing one shared mute value.

Verified in the bulk panel implementation that mixed numeric fields initialize to an empty draft with a Mixed placeholder, and commitNumber returns early for blank blur and Enter events, so focus and blur alone do not apply unintended changes.

Verified with the pruneSelectionRef bridge probe that applying a reloaded valid-id set while multiple clips are selected removes stale ids and reassigns primaryClipId to the remaining clip, leaving no orphaned references.
